chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: It needs a first message with a new topic

chat.author: Kyle Rose
chat.text: Where does the raw AI transcript end up?

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @Kyle Rose do you mean the one we're doing for the session here?

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: They become part of the session recording

chat.author: Kyle Rose
chat.text: Sorry, what I was going to say was that the existing MC-QUIC design achieves reliability via unicast recovery, not (e.g.) FEC. It's not a final design, and we have of course considered FEC as an enhancement, but it's not clear whether that would be MC-specific or if we should work collectively on an FEC layer for QUIC more generally.

chat.author: Kyle Rose
chat.text: There's a lot about this that is still a demonstration of what the technology can achieve rather than some polished spec and implementation.

chat.author: Behcet Sarikaya
chat.text: Folks I saw aircast on App Store but there are so many of them, which one?

chat.author: Omar Ramadan
chat.text: We've got a Linux v6 AMT relay implementation, lets chat!

chat.author: Sanjay Gupta
chat.text:
use this link to get the Aircast Live app -- https://apps.apple.com/us/app/aircast-live/id1513908670
Aircast Live

chat.author: Sanjay Gupta
chat.text: Ot is great that we are building a community finally. Happy to support with content when feasible

chat.author: Mohamed Boucadair
chat.text: @Franck: for the AMT over QUIC, there is an effort to have  some operational guidane on foo-over-QUIC proposals. A first recap will be shared in opsarea this week: https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/126/materials/agenda-126-opsarea.
